timeline_description: Major milestones in the Cold War competition between the Soviet Union and the United States in space exploration.
title: Sputnik 1 is launched
description: The Soviet Union launches Sputnik 1, the first artificial satellite, opening the Space Race.
location: Baikonur Cosmodrome, Kazakh SSR, Soviet Union

timeline_description: Major milestones in the Cold War competition between the Soviet Union and the United States in space exploration.
title: Sputnik 2 carries Laika into orbit
description: The Soviet Union sends Laika into orbit aboard Sputnik 2, demonstrating a second rapid space triumph.
location: Baikonur Cosmodrome, Kazakh SSR, Soviet Union

timeline_description: Major milestones in the Cold War competition between the Soviet Union and the United States in space exploration.
title: Explorer 1 reaches orbit
description: The United States launches Explorer 1, its first successful satellite, and enters the contest in earnest.

timeline_description: Major milestones in the Cold War competition between the Soviet Union and the United States in space exploration.
title: NASA is established
description: President Dwight D. Eisenhower signs the National Aeronautics and Space Act, creating NASA.

timeline_description: Major milestones in the Cold War competition between the Soviet Union and the United States in space exploration.
title: Luna 2 reaches the Moon
description: The Soviet Luna 2 mission becomes the first human-made object to impact the Moon.

timeline_description: Major milestones in the Cold War competition between the Soviet Union and the United States in space exploration.
title: Yuri Gagarin orbits Earth
description: Vostok 1 makes Yuri Gagarin the first human in space and the first person to orbit Earth.
location: Baikonur Cosmodrome, Kazakh SSR, Soviet Union

timeline_description: Major milestones in the Cold War competition between the Soviet Union and the United States in space exploration.
title: Alan Shepard flies in Mercury-Redstone 3
description: Alan Shepard becomes the first American in space on a suborbital flight.

timeline_description: Major milestones in the Cold War competition between the Soviet Union and the United States in space exploration.
title: Kennedy commits the United States to the Moon
description: President John F. Kennedy asks Congress to support landing a man on the Moon before the decade ends.

timeline_description: Major milestones in the Cold War competition between the Soviet Union and the United States in space exploration.
title: John Glenn becomes first American to orbit Earth
description: Friendship 7 carries John Glenn into orbit, a key American achievement in crewed flight.

timeline_description: Major milestones in the Cold War competition between the Soviet Union and the United States in space exploration.
title: Valentina Tereshkova becomes first woman in space
description: Vostok 6 carries Valentina Tereshkova into orbit, marking another Soviet first.
location: Baikonur Cosmodrome, Kazakh SSR, Soviet Union

timeline_description: Major milestones in the Cold War competition between the Soviet Union and the United States in space exploration.
title: Voskhod 1 carries first multi-person crew
description: The Soviet Voskhod 1 mission carries three cosmonauts, the first spaceflight with more than one crew member.
location: Baikonur Cosmodrome, Kazakh SSR, Soviet Union

timeline_description: Major milestones in the Cold War competition between the Soviet Union and the United States in space exploration.
title: Alexei Leonov completes first spacewalk
description: Voskhod 2 makes Leonov the first person to conduct an extravehicular activity.

timeline_description: Major milestones in the Cold War competition between the Soviet Union and the United States in space exploration.
title: Gemini 6A and Gemini 7 perform rendezvous
description: The United States demonstrates controlled orbital rendezvous, an essential step toward lunar missions.

timeline_description: Major milestones in the Cold War competition between the Soviet Union and the United States in space exploration.
title: Luna 9 makes first soft landing on the Moon
description: The Soviet Luna 9 mission achieves the first successful soft landing on the lunar surface.

timeline_description: Major milestones in the Cold War competition between the Soviet Union and the United States in space exploration.
title: Gemini 8 completes first docking in orbit
description: Astronauts Neil Armstrong and David Scott carry out the first successful docking of two spacecraft in orbit.

timeline_description: Major milestones in the Cold War competition between the Soviet Union and the United States in space exploration.
title: Apollo 1 fire kills three astronauts
description: A launch-pad fire during a ground test kills the Apollo 1 crew and forces major NASA redesigns.

timeline_description: Major milestones in the Cold War competition between the Soviet Union and the United States in space exploration.
title: Apollo 8 orbits the Moon
description: Apollo 8 becomes the first crewed mission to leave Earth orbit, reach the Moon, and return safely.

timeline_description: Major milestones in the Cold War competition between the Soviet Union and the United States in space exploration.
title: Apollo 11 lands on the Moon
description: Neil Armstrong and Buzz Aldrin land on the Moon, the defining achievement of the Space Race.

timeline_description: Major milestones in the Cold War competition between the Soviet Union and the United States in space exploration.
title: Salyut 1 becomes first space station
description: The Soviet Union launches Salyut 1, the world’s first space station.

timeline_description: Major milestones in the Cold War competition between the Soviet Union and the United States in space exploration.
title: Apollo-Soyuz mission symbolizes détente
description: American and Soviet crews dock in orbit during the Apollo-Soyuz Test Project, widely seen as the end of the Space Race era.

timeline_description: Chronological milestones in the transformation of European natural philosophy into modern science during the 16th and 17th centuries.
title: Copernicus publishes De revolutionibus
description: Nicolaus Copernicus publishes a heliocentric model that challenges the long-dominant geocentric cosmos.

timeline_description: Chronological milestones in the transformation of European natural philosophy into modern science during the 16th and 17th centuries.
title: Vesalius publishes De humani corporis fabrica
description: Andreas Vesalius advances anatomical study through direct observation and richly illustrated human dissection.

timeline_description: Chronological milestones in the transformation of European natural philosophy into modern science during the 16th and 17th centuries.
title: Tycho Brahe observes a new star
description: Tycho Brahe’s observation of a supernova undermines the Aristotelian idea of an unchanging heavens.

timeline_description: Chronological milestones in the transformation of European natural philosophy into modern science during the 16th and 17th centuries.
title: Gregorian calendar reform is introduced
description: Pope Gregory XIII promulgates a new calendar based on more accurate astronomical calculation.

timeline_description: Chronological milestones in the transformation of European natural philosophy into modern science during the 16th and 17th centuries.
title: William Gilbert publishes De Magnete
description: Gilbert’s study of magnetism becomes a landmark in experimental natural philosophy.

timeline_description: Chronological milestones in the transformation of European natural philosophy into modern science during the 16th and 17th centuries.
title: Kepler publishes Astronomia nova
description: Johannes Kepler proposes laws of planetary motion that replace circular perfection with elliptical orbits.

timeline_description: Chronological milestones in the transformation of European natural philosophy into modern science during the 16th and 17th centuries.
title: Galileo publishes Sidereus Nuncius
description: Galileo reports telescopic discoveries including Jupiter’s moons, strengthening the case for heliocentrism.

timeline_description: Chronological milestones in the transformation of European natural philosophy into modern science during the 16th and 17th centuries.
title: Francis Bacon publishes Novum Organum
description: Bacon argues for systematic observation and induction, influencing later scientific method.

timeline_description: Chronological milestones in the transformation of European natural philosophy into modern science during the 16th and 17th centuries.
title: Harvey explains the circulation of blood
description: William Harvey’s De Motu Cordis redefines physiology by describing blood circulation and the heart’s pumping action.

timeline_description: Chronological milestones in the transformation of European natural philosophy into modern science during the 16th and 17th centuries.
title: Galileo publishes Dialogue Concerning the Two Chief World Systems
description: Galileo’s dialogue forcefully compares Copernican and Ptolemaic cosmologies and intensifies conflict with church authorities.

timeline_description: Chronological milestones in the transformation of European natural philosophy into modern science during the 16th and 17th centuries.
title: Descartes publishes Discourse on Method
description: René Descartes links mathematics, rational inquiry, and natural philosophy in a text central to early modern science.

timeline_description: Chronological milestones in the transformation of European natural philosophy into modern science during the 16th and 17th centuries.
title: Torricelli invents the barometer
description: Evangelista Torricelli demonstrates atmospheric pressure with the mercury barometer.

timeline_description: Chronological milestones in the transformation of European natural philosophy into modern science during the 16th and 17th centuries.
title: Huygens identifies Titan
description: Christiaan Huygens discovers Saturn’s moon Titan, illustrating the growing power of telescopic astronomy.

timeline_description: Chronological milestones in the transformation of European natural philosophy into modern science during the 16th and 17th centuries.
title: Royal Society is founded
description: The Royal Society of London becomes one of the leading institutions for organized scientific exchange.

timeline_description: Chronological milestones in the transformation of European natural philosophy into modern science during the 16th and 17th centuries.
title: Boyle’s law is formulated
description: Robert Boyle establishes a quantitative relationship between the pressure and volume of gases.

timeline_description: Chronological milestones in the transformation of European natural philosophy into modern science during the 16th and 17th centuries.
title: Hooke publishes Micrographia
description: Robert Hooke’s richly illustrated microscopy book popularizes close observation and introduces the term cell.

timeline_description: Chronological milestones in the transformation of European natural philosophy into modern science during the 16th and 17th centuries.
title: French Academy of Sciences is founded
description: The Académie Royale des Sciences strengthens state-backed scientific research in France.

timeline_description: Chronological milestones in the transformation of European natural philosophy into modern science during the 16th and 17th centuries.
title: Rømer estimates the speed of light
description: Ole Rømer uses astronomical observations to show that light travels at a finite speed.

timeline_description: Chronological milestones in the transformation of European natural philosophy into modern science during the 16th and 17th centuries.
title: Newton publishes Principia
description: Isaac Newton unifies terrestrial and celestial mechanics in Philosophiae Naturalis Principia Mathematica.

timeline_description: Chronological milestones in the transformation of European natural philosophy into modern science during the 16th and 17th centuries.
title: Newton publishes Opticks
description: Newton’s Opticks extends the revolution through experimental work on light and color.

timeline_description: Major campaigns, turning points, and political settlements in the medieval Crusades to the eastern Mediterranean and related crusading movements.
title: Pope Urban II calls for the First Crusade
description: At the Council of Clermont, Pope Urban II urges western Christians to aid Byzantium and recover Jerusalem.

timeline_description: Major campaigns, turning points, and political settlements in the medieval Crusades to the eastern Mediterranean and related crusading movements.
title: People’s Crusade marches east
description: Unorganized popular forces depart for the Holy Land before the main princely armies, with disastrous results.

timeline_description: Major campaigns, turning points, and political settlements in the medieval Crusades to the eastern Mediterranean and related crusading movements.
title: Crusaders capture Nicaea
description: The First Crusade wins its first major success with the surrender of Nicaea.

timeline_description: Major campaigns, turning points, and political settlements in the medieval Crusades to the eastern Mediterranean and related crusading movements.
title: Antioch falls to the First Crusade
description: After a long siege, crusader forces take Antioch and hold it against counterattack.

timeline_description: Major campaigns, turning points, and political settlements in the medieval Crusades to the eastern Mediterranean and related crusading movements.
title: Jerusalem is captured
description: Crusader armies take Jerusalem and establish Latin Christian rule in the city.

timeline_description: Major campaigns, turning points, and political settlements in the medieval Crusades to the eastern Mediterranean and related crusading movements.
title: Kingdom of Jerusalem is consolidated
description: The Latin Kingdom of Jerusalem emerges as the leading crusader state in the Levant.

timeline_description: Major campaigns, turning points, and political settlements in the medieval Crusades to the eastern Mediterranean and related crusading movements.
title: Edessa falls to Zengi
description: The loss of Edessa shocks Latin Christendom and prompts calls for a new crusade.

timeline_description: Major campaigns, turning points, and political settlements in the medieval Crusades to the eastern Mediterranean and related crusading movements.
title: Second Crusade begins
description: European rulers including Louis VII of France and Conrad III of Germany lead a new crusade eastward.

timeline_description: Major campaigns, turning points, and political settlements in the medieval Crusades to the eastern Mediterranean and related crusading movements.
title: Second Crusade collapses
description: The failure of the campaign, especially at Damascus, weakens crusader prestige.

timeline_description: Major campaigns, turning points, and political settlements in the medieval Crusades to the eastern Mediterranean and related crusading movements.
title: Saladin retakes Jerusalem
description: After the crusader defeat at Hattin, Saladin captures Jerusalem and transforms the political balance.

timeline_description: Major campaigns, turning points, and political settlements in the medieval Crusades to the eastern Mediterranean and related crusading movements.
title: Third Crusade begins
description: Richard I of England, Philip II of France, and Frederick I Barbarossa answer the loss of Jerusalem.

timeline_description: Major campaigns, turning points, and political settlements in the medieval Crusades to the eastern Mediterranean and related crusading movements.
title: Acre is recovered by crusaders
description: After a long siege, crusader forces retake Acre, securing a vital coastal base.

timeline_description: Major campaigns, turning points, and political settlements in the medieval Crusades to the eastern Mediterranean and related crusading movements.
title: Treaty of Jaffa ends the Third Crusade
description: Richard I and Saladin agree to a settlement allowing Christian pilgrim access to Jerusalem while leaving the city under Muslim rule.

timeline_description: Major campaigns, turning points, and political settlements in the medieval Crusades to the eastern Mediterranean and related crusading movements.
title: Fourth Crusade is launched
description: A new crusade begins but is diverted by debt, politics, and Venetian interests.

timeline_description: Major campaigns, turning points, and political settlements in the medieval Crusades to the eastern Mediterranean and related crusading movements.
title: Crusaders sack Constantinople
description: The Fourth Crusade captures and sacks Constantinople, deepening the divide between Latin and Greek Christianity.

timeline_description: Major campaigns, turning points, and political settlements in the medieval Crusades to the eastern Mediterranean and related crusading movements.
title: Children’s Crusade tradition emerges
description: A popular but ill-fated crusading movement enters European memory and legend.

timeline_description: Major campaigns, turning points, and political settlements in the medieval Crusades to the eastern Mediterranean and related crusading movements.
title: Fifth Crusade begins
description: Crusader strategy shifts toward Egypt, seen as the key to control of Jerusalem.

timeline_description: Major campaigns, turning points, and political settlements in the medieval Crusades to the eastern Mediterranean and related crusading movements.
title: Jerusalem is regained by treaty
description: Emperor Frederick II secures Jerusalem through negotiation rather than major battle during the Sixth Crusade.

timeline_description: Major campaigns, turning points, and political settlements in the medieval Crusades to the eastern Mediterranean and related crusading movements.
title: Jerusalem is lost again
description: Khwarazmian forces seize Jerusalem, ending the city’s brief restoration to crusader control.

timeline_description: Major campaigns, turning points, and political settlements in the medieval Crusades to the eastern Mediterranean and related crusading movements.
title: Acre falls and the crusader era ends in the Levant
description: The Mamluk capture of Acre destroys the last major mainland crusader stronghold in the Holy Land.

timeline_description: Key economic shocks, policy responses, and social turning points during the worldwide Great Depression.
title: Black Thursday hits Wall Street
description: Heavy selling on the New York Stock Exchange signals the beginning of the stock market crash.

timeline_description: Key economic shocks, policy responses, and social turning points during the worldwide Great Depression.
title: Black Tuesday deepens the crash
description: Panic selling intensifies, making October 29 the emblematic day of the market collapse.

timeline_description: Key economic shocks, policy responses, and social turning points during the worldwide Great Depression.
title: Smoot-Hawley Tariff Act becomes law
description: The United States sharply raises tariffs, worsening international trade tensions during the downturn.

timeline_description: Key economic shocks, policy responses, and social turning points during the worldwide Great Depression.
title: Bank of United States fails
description: The collapse of a major New York bank becomes a symbol of the banking crisis.

timeline_description: Key economic shocks, policy responses, and social turning points during the worldwide Great Depression.
title: Credit-Anstalt collapses
description: The failure of Austria’s Credit-Anstalt accelerates financial panic in Europe.

timeline_description: Key economic shocks, policy responses, and social turning points during the worldwide Great Depression.
title: Britain leaves the gold standard
description: Britain suspends gold convertibility, marking a major break in interwar monetary policy.

timeline_description: Key economic shocks, policy responses, and social turning points during the worldwide Great Depression.
title: Bonus Army is dispersed
description: U.S. veterans demanding early bonus payments are forcibly removed from Washington, exposing social distress.

timeline_description: Key economic shocks, policy responses, and social turning points during the worldwide Great Depression.
title: Franklin D. Roosevelt is elected president
description: Roosevelt’s election opens the way for a more activist federal response to the Depression.

timeline_description: Key economic shocks, policy responses, and social turning points during the worldwide Great Depression.
title: Roosevelt takes office
description: Franklin D. Roosevelt begins his presidency with promises of bold experimentation against the economic crisis.

timeline_description: Key economic shocks, policy responses, and social turning points during the worldwide Great Depression.
title: National bank holiday is declared
description: Roosevelt temporarily closes U.S. banks to halt runs and stabilize the financial system.

timeline_description: Key economic shocks, policy responses, and social turning points during the worldwide Great Depression.
title: Civilian Conservation Corps is created
description: The CCC becomes one of the first major New Deal relief and employment programs.

timeline_description: Key economic shocks, policy responses, and social turning points during the worldwide Great Depression.
title: Glass-Steagall Act is enacted
description: Banking reform creates federal deposit insurance and separates commercial from investment banking.

timeline_description: Key economic shocks, policy responses, and social turning points during the worldwide Great Depression.
title: National Industrial Recovery Act becomes law
description: The NIRA attempts industrial recovery through codes, public works, and labor provisions.

timeline_description: Key economic shocks, policy responses, and social turning points during the worldwide Great Depression.
title: Dust Bowl conditions worsen
description: Ecological disaster and drought intensify economic hardship across the Great Plains.

timeline_description: Key economic shocks, policy responses, and social turning points during the worldwide Great Depression.
title: Works Progress Administration is created
description: The WPA expands federal job creation on an enormous scale.

timeline_description: Key economic shocks, policy responses, and social turning points during the worldwide Great Depression.
title: Social Security Act is signed
description: The act establishes old-age pensions, unemployment insurance, and a lasting welfare framework.

timeline_description: Key economic shocks, policy responses, and social turning points during the worldwide Great Depression.
title: Wagner Act strengthens labor rights
description: The National Labor Relations Act protects union organizing and collective bargaining.

timeline_description: Key economic shocks, policy responses, and social turning points during the worldwide Great Depression.
title: Keynes publishes The General Theory
description: John Maynard Keynes provides a new framework for understanding mass unemployment and state intervention.

timeline_description: Key economic shocks, policy responses, and social turning points during the worldwide Great Depression.
title: The recession of 1937–38 begins
description: A sharp downturn interrupts recovery and reveals the Depression’s continuing fragility.

timeline_description: Key economic shocks, policy responses, and social turning points during the worldwide Great Depression.
title: World War II begins in Europe
description: The outbreak of war helps bring the Depression era to a close through rearmament and mobilization.

timeline_title: Timeline of the History of China
timeline_description: Major dynastic, political, and geopolitical turning points in Chinese history from the Bronze Age to the reform era.
title: Shang dynasty emerges
description: The Shang becomes the first Chinese dynasty known from extensive written records and archaeological evidence.

timeline_title: Timeline of the History of China
timeline_description: Major dynastic, political, and geopolitical turning points in Chinese history from the Bronze Age to the reform era.
title: Zhou defeats the Shang
description: The Zhou conquest establishes a new dynasty and the enduring concept of the Mandate of Heaven.

timeline_title: Timeline of the History of China
timeline_description: Major dynastic, political, and geopolitical turning points in Chinese history from the Bronze Age to the reform era.
title: Eastern Zhou period begins
description: The Zhou court moves east after invasions, beginning an era of weakened royal authority and regional rivalry.

timeline_title: Timeline of the History of China
timeline_description: Major dynastic, political, and geopolitical turning points in Chinese history from the Bronze Age to the reform era.
title: Qin unifies China
description: Qin Shi Huang ends the Warring States period and creates the first centralized imperial Chinese state.

timeline_title: Timeline of the History of China
timeline_description: Major dynastic, political, and geopolitical turning points in Chinese history from the Bronze Age to the reform era.
title: Han dynasty is founded
description: Liu Bang establishes the Han dynasty, which becomes one of the formative periods of imperial China.

timeline_title: Timeline of the History of China
timeline_description: Major dynastic, political, and geopolitical turning points in Chinese history from the Bronze Age to the reform era.
title: Wang Mang establishes the Xin dynasty
description: A brief interregnum interrupts Han rule and triggers major political upheaval.

timeline_title: Timeline of the History of China
timeline_description: Major dynastic, political, and geopolitical turning points in Chinese history from the Bronze Age to the reform era.
title: Han dynasty falls
description: The collapse of the Han ushers in the era of the Three Kingdoms and prolonged division.

timeline_title: Timeline of the History of China
timeline_description: Major dynastic, political, and geopolitical turning points in Chinese history from the Bronze Age to the reform era.
title: Sui reunifies China
description: The Sui dynasty reunites north and south after centuries of fragmentation.

timeline_title: Timeline of the History of China
timeline_description: Major dynastic, political, and geopolitical turning points in Chinese history from the Bronze Age to the reform era.
title: Tang dynasty begins
description: The Tang dynasty is founded, inaugurating one of the most influential periods in Chinese history.

timeline_title: Timeline of the History of China
timeline_description: Major dynastic, political, and geopolitical turning points in Chinese history from the Bronze Age to the reform era.
title: An Lushan Rebellion begins
description: A massive rebellion weakens the Tang state and permanently alters imperial governance.

timeline_title: Timeline of the History of China
timeline_description: Major dynastic, political, and geopolitical turning points in Chinese history from the Bronze Age to the reform era.
title: Tang dynasty ends
description: The fall of the Tang opens the Five Dynasties and Ten Kingdoms period.

timeline_title: Timeline of the History of China
timeline_description: Major dynastic, political, and geopolitical turning points in Chinese history from the Bronze Age to the reform era.
title: Song dynasty is founded
description: The Song reunifies much of China and presides over major commercial and technological growth.

timeline_title: Timeline of the History of China
timeline_description: Major dynastic, political, and geopolitical turning points in Chinese history from the Bronze Age to the reform era.
title: Kublai Khan proclaims the Yuan dynasty
description: The Mongol ruler establishes the Yuan dynasty in China.

timeline_title: Timeline of the History of China
timeline_description: Major dynastic, political, and geopolitical turning points in Chinese history from the Bronze Age to the reform era.
title: Ming dynasty is founded
description: The Ming overthrow Mongol rule and restore Han-led imperial government.

timeline_title: Timeline of the History of China
timeline_description: Major dynastic, political, and geopolitical turning points in Chinese history from the Bronze Age to the reform era.
title: Zheng He begins his first voyage
description: The Ming admiral launches the first of the great treasure voyages across the Indian Ocean.

timeline_title: Timeline of the History of China
timeline_description: Major dynastic, political, and geopolitical turning points in Chinese history from the Bronze Age to the reform era.
title: Qing dynasty takes Beijing
description: Manchu forces enter Beijing and establish Qing rule over China.

timeline_title: Timeline of the History of China
timeline_description: Major dynastic, political, and geopolitical turning points in Chinese history from the Bronze Age to the reform era.
title: Xinhai Revolution begins
description: Revolutionary uprisings topple the Qing and end more than two millennia of imperial rule.

timeline_title: Timeline of the History of China
timeline_description: Major dynastic, political, and geopolitical turning points in Chinese history from the Bronze Age to the reform era.
title: Republic of China is proclaimed
description: The Republic of China replaces the Qing dynasty as China’s new national government.

timeline_title: Timeline of the History of China
timeline_description: Major dynastic, political, and geopolitical turning points in Chinese history from the Bronze Age to the reform era.
title: People’s Republic of China is proclaimed
description: Mao Zedong declares the founding of the People’s Republic of China after Communist victory in the civil war.

timeline_title: Timeline of the History of China
timeline_description: Major dynastic, political, and geopolitical turning points in Chinese history from the Bronze Age to the reform era.
title: Reform and opening begins
description: Deng Xiaoping’s rise and the Third Plenum launch market-oriented reforms and a new development era.

timeline_title: Timeline of the American Civil War
timeline_description: Political decisions, military campaigns, and legal turning points in the United States Civil War.
title: Abraham Lincoln is elected president
description: Lincoln’s election intensifies sectional conflict over slavery and secession.

timeline_title: Timeline of the American Civil War
timeline_description: Political decisions, military campaigns, and legal turning points in the United States Civil War.
title: South Carolina secedes
description: South Carolina becomes the first state to leave the Union after Lincoln’s election.

timeline_title: Timeline of the American Civil War
timeline_description: Political decisions, military campaigns, and legal turning points in the United States Civil War.
title: Confederate States of America is formed
description: Seceding Southern states establish the Confederate government at Montgomery.

timeline_title: Timeline of the American Civil War
timeline_description: Political decisions, military campaigns, and legal turning points in the United States Civil War.
title: Fort Sumter is attacked
description: Confederate bombardment of Fort Sumter begins the Civil War.

timeline_title: Timeline of the American Civil War
timeline_description: Political decisions, military campaigns, and legal turning points in the United States Civil War.
title: First Battle of Bull Run is fought
description: The first major land battle ends in a Confederate victory and shatters hopes of a short war.

timeline_title: Timeline of the American Civil War
timeline_description: Political decisions, military campaigns, and legal turning points in the United States Civil War.
title: Fort Donelson falls to Union forces
description: Ulysses S. Grant wins an important western victory and becomes a national figure.

timeline_title: Timeline of the American Civil War
timeline_description: Political decisions, military campaigns, and legal turning points in the United States Civil War.
title: Monitor and Merrimack fight at Hampton Roads
description: The clash of ironclads signals a new age in naval warfare.

timeline_title: Timeline of the American Civil War
timeline_description: Political decisions, military campaigns, and legal turning points in the United States Civil War.
title: Battle of Shiloh begins
description: One of the war’s bloodiest early battles reveals the scale of the coming conflict.

timeline_title: Timeline of the American Civil War
timeline_description: Political decisions, military campaigns, and legal turning points in the United States Civil War.
title: Battle of Antietam is fought
description: The bloodiest single day in American military history halts Lee’s advance into the North.

timeline_title: Timeline of the American Civil War
timeline_description: Political decisions, military campaigns, and legal turning points in the United States Civil War.
title: Lincoln issues the preliminary Emancipation Proclamation
description: Lincoln announces that enslaved people in rebellious states will be declared free if the Confederacy does not return to the Union.

timeline_title: Timeline of the American Civil War
timeline_description: Political decisions, military campaigns, and legal turning points in the United States Civil War.
title: Emancipation Proclamation takes effect
description: Lincoln’s proclamation transforms the war by making emancipation a central Union war aim.

timeline_title: Timeline of the American Civil War
timeline_description: Political decisions, military campaigns, and legal turning points in the United States Civil War.
title: Siege of Vicksburg begins
description: Union operations against Vicksburg target Confederate control of the Mississippi River.

timeline_title: Timeline of the American Civil War
timeline_description: Political decisions, military campaigns, and legal turning points in the United States Civil War.
title: Battle of Gettysburg begins
description: The war’s best-known battle starts as Confederate forces invade Pennsylvania.

timeline_title: Timeline of the American Civil War
timeline_description: Political decisions, military campaigns, and legal turning points in the United States Civil War.
title: Vicksburg surrenders
description: The fall of Vicksburg gives the Union effective control of the Mississippi River.

timeline_title: Timeline of the American Civil War
timeline_description: Political decisions, military campaigns, and legal turning points in the United States Civil War.
title: Lincoln delivers the Gettysburg Address
description: Lincoln redefines the war as a struggle for national renewal and equality.

timeline_title: Timeline of the American Civil War
timeline_description: Political decisions, military campaigns, and legal turning points in the United States Civil War.
title: Grant launches the Overland Campaign
description: Union armies begin sustained operations against Robert E. Lee in Virginia.

timeline_title: Timeline of the American Civil War
timeline_description: Political decisions, military campaigns, and legal turning points in the United States Civil War.
title: Atlanta falls to Sherman
description: The capture of Atlanta boosts Northern morale and aids Lincoln’s reelection.

timeline_title: Timeline of the American Civil War
timeline_description: Political decisions, military campaigns, and legal turning points in the United States Civil War.
title: Sherman begins the March to the Sea
description: Sherman’s campaign through Georgia demonstrates the Union’s strategy of hard war.

timeline_title: Timeline of the American Civil War
timeline_description: Political decisions, military campaigns, and legal turning points in the United States Civil War.
title: Lee surrenders at Appomattox
description: Robert E. Lee surrenders the Army of Northern Virginia to Grant, marking the effective end of the war in the east.
location: Appomattox Court House, Virginia, United States

timeline_title: Timeline of the American Civil War
timeline_description: Political decisions, military campaigns, and legal turning points in the United States Civil War.
title: Thirteenth Amendment is ratified
description: The amendment abolishes slavery throughout the United States.

timeline_description: Major stylistic, institutional, and recording milestones in the history of jazz from its origins to its global recognition.
title: Maple Leaf Rag is published
description: Scott Joplin’s famous ragtime composition reflects one of the key musical streams feeding early jazz.

timeline_description: Major stylistic, institutional, and recording milestones in the history of jazz from its origins to its global recognition.
title: Buddy Bolden becomes a formative New Orleans bandleader
description: Charles “Buddy” Bolden emerges in later accounts as a foundational figure in the development of early jazz style.

timeline_description: Major stylistic, institutional, and recording milestones in the history of jazz from its origins to its global recognition.
title: The Great Migration reshapes Black musical culture
description: Large-scale movement of African Americans from the South helps spread jazz beyond New Orleans.

timeline_description: Major stylistic, institutional, and recording milestones in the history of jazz from its origins to its global recognition.
title: Original Dixieland Jass Band makes first jazz recordings
description: Commercial recording helps popularize the new music nationally and internationally.

timeline_description: Major stylistic, institutional, and recording milestones in the history of jazz from its origins to its global recognition.
title: Storyville closes
description: The shutdown of New Orleans’s Storyville district contributes to the dispersal of local musicians.

timeline_description: Major stylistic, institutional, and recording milestones in the history of jazz from its origins to its global recognition.
title: King Oliver’s Creole Jazz Band moves to Chicago
description: Chicago becomes a major center for early jazz performance and recording.

timeline_description: Major stylistic, institutional, and recording milestones in the history of jazz from its origins to its global recognition.
title: Louis Armstrong begins Hot Five recordings
description: Armstrong’s recordings help establish the soloist as the central creative force in jazz.

timeline_description: Major stylistic, institutional, and recording milestones in the history of jazz from its origins to its global recognition.
title: Duke Ellington starts Cotton Club residency
description: Ellington’s orchestra gains national prominence through radio broadcasts from Harlem.

timeline_description: Major stylistic, institutional, and recording milestones in the history of jazz from its origins to its global recognition.
title: Benny Goodman’s success marks the swing era
description: Goodman’s breakthrough helps turn swing into the dominant popular jazz style of the period.

timeline_description: Major stylistic, institutional, and recording milestones in the history of jazz from its origins to its global recognition.
title: Goodman plays Carnegie Hall
description: The Carnegie Hall concert becomes one of the most celebrated events in jazz history.

timeline_description: Major stylistic, institutional, and recording milestones in the history of jazz from its origins to its global recognition.
title: Minton’s Playhouse fosters bebop innovation
description: Jam sessions in Harlem help incubate the complex harmonic and rhythmic language of bebop.

timeline_description: Major stylistic, institutional, and recording milestones in the history of jazz from its origins to its global recognition.
title: Bebop takes shape on record
description: Recordings by Dizzy Gillespie and Charlie Parker announce a new modern jazz language.

timeline_description: Major stylistic, institutional, and recording milestones in the history of jazz from its origins to its global recognition.
title: Miles Davis records Birth of the Cool sessions
description: The sessions point toward cool jazz and a new chamber-like ensemble sound.

timeline_description: Major stylistic, institutional, and recording milestones in the history of jazz from its origins to its global recognition.
title: Ellington triumphs at Newport
description: Duke Ellington’s Newport Jazz Festival appearance revives his public standing and becomes a famous festival moment.

timeline_description: Major stylistic, institutional, and recording milestones in the history of jazz from its origins to its global recognition.
title: Kind of Blue is released
description: Miles Davis’s modal jazz landmark becomes one of the most influential albums in any genre.

timeline_description: Major stylistic, institutional, and recording milestones in the history of jazz from its origins to its global recognition.
title: Time Out popularizes odd-meter jazz
description: The Dave Brubeck Quartet reaches a broad audience with an album built around unusual time signatures.

timeline_description: Major stylistic, institutional, and recording milestones in the history of jazz from its origins to its global recognition.
title: Ornette Coleman releases Free Jazz
description: Coleman’s work becomes a defining statement for the free jazz movement.

timeline_description: Major stylistic, institutional, and recording milestones in the history of jazz from its origins to its global recognition.
title: A Love Supreme is recorded
description: John Coltrane’s suite becomes one of the central spiritual and artistic statements in jazz.
location: Englewood Cliffs, New Jersey, United States

timeline_description: Major stylistic, institutional, and recording milestones in the history of jazz from its origins to its global recognition.
title: In a Silent Way launches jazz fusion era
description: Miles Davis’s recording points toward the fusion of jazz improvisation with rock-based textures and rhythms.

timeline_description: Major stylistic, institutional, and recording milestones in the history of jazz from its origins to its global recognition.
title: UNESCO inaugurates International Jazz Day
description: The United Nations cultural agency gives jazz an annual global celebration reflecting its worldwide status.

timeline_title: Timeline of the Women's Rights Movement
timeline_description: Major legal, intellectual, and activist milestones in the modern women’s rights movement, especially from the mid-20th century onward.
title: The Second Sex is published
description: Simone de Beauvoir’s book becomes a foundational text for modern feminist thought.

timeline_title: Timeline of the Women's Rights Movement
timeline_description: Major legal, intellectual, and activist milestones in the modern women’s rights movement, especially from the mid-20th century onward.
title: The oral contraceptive pill is approved in the United States
description: The pill reshapes debates over bodily autonomy, family planning, and women’s social roles.

timeline_title: Timeline of the Women's Rights Movement
timeline_description: Major legal, intellectual, and activist milestones in the modern women’s rights movement, especially from the mid-20th century onward.
title: The Feminine Mystique is published
description: Betty Friedan’s book helps catalyze a new phase of organized feminist activism in the United States.

timeline_title: Timeline of the Women's Rights Movement
timeline_description: Major legal, intellectual, and activist milestones in the modern women’s rights movement, especially from the mid-20th century onward.
title: Equal Pay Act becomes law
description: The United States bans wage discrimination based on sex for many workers covered by federal law.

timeline_title: Timeline of the Women's Rights Movement
timeline_description: Major legal, intellectual, and activist milestones in the modern women’s rights movement, especially from the mid-20th century onward.
title: Title VII prohibits sex discrimination in employment
description: The Civil Rights Act bars workplace discrimination on the basis of sex as well as race, religion, and national origin.

timeline_title: Timeline of the Women's Rights Movement
timeline_description: Major legal, intellectual, and activist milestones in the modern women’s rights movement, especially from the mid-20th century onward.
title: National Organization for Women is founded
description: NOW becomes one of the most influential organizations in the modern U.S. women’s movement.

timeline_title: Timeline of the Women's Rights Movement
timeline_description: Major legal, intellectual, and activist milestones in the modern women’s rights movement, especially from the mid-20th century onward.
title: Miss America protest draws national attention
description: Demonstrators in Atlantic City turn the pageant into a symbol of protest against restrictive gender norms.
location: Atlantic City, New Jersey, United States

timeline_title: Timeline of the Women's Rights Movement
timeline_description: Major legal, intellectual, and activist milestones in the modern women’s rights movement, especially from the mid-20th century onward.
title: Women’s Strike for Equality is held
description: Large demonstrations mark the fiftieth anniversary of U.S. suffrage and demand equality in work, education, and family life.

timeline_title: Timeline of the Women's Rights Movement
timeline_description: Major legal, intellectual, and activist milestones in the modern women’s rights movement, especially from the mid-20th century onward.
title: Equal Rights Amendment passes Congress
description: Congress approves the ERA and sends it to the states, though it is never ratified.

timeline_title: Timeline of the Women's Rights Movement
timeline_description: Major legal, intellectual, and activist milestones in the modern women’s rights movement, especially from the mid-20th century onward.
title: Title IX becomes law
description: Title IX prohibits sex discrimination in federally funded education programs.

timeline_title: Timeline of the Women's Rights Movement
timeline_description: Major legal, intellectual, and activist milestones in the modern women’s rights movement, especially from the mid-20th century onward.
title: Roe v. Wade is decided
description: The U.S. Supreme Court recognizes a constitutional right to abortion, making reproductive rights central to women’s-rights politics.

timeline_title: Timeline of the Women's Rights Movement
timeline_description: Major legal, intellectual, and activist milestones in the modern women’s rights movement, especially from the mid-20th century onward.
title: UN declares International Women’s Year
description: The United Nations elevates women’s rights on the global agenda and convenes a major conference in Mexico City.

timeline_title: Timeline of the Women's Rights Movement
timeline_description: Major legal, intellectual, and activist milestones in the modern women’s rights movement, especially from the mid-20th century onward.
title: CEDAW is adopted
description: The UN General Assembly adopts the Convention on the Elimination of All Forms of Discrimination against Women.

timeline_title: Timeline of the Women's Rights Movement
timeline_description: Major legal, intellectual, and activist milestones in the modern women’s rights movement, especially from the mid-20th century onward.
title: CEDAW enters into force
description: The convention becomes binding for ratifying states, creating a major international framework for women’s rights.

timeline_title: Timeline of the Women's Rights Movement
timeline_description: Major legal, intellectual, and activist milestones in the modern women’s rights movement, especially from the mid-20th century onward.
title: Violence Against Women Act is signed
description: The United States expands federal tools to address domestic violence and sexual assault.

timeline_title: Timeline of the Women's Rights Movement
timeline_description: Major legal, intellectual, and activist milestones in the modern women’s rights movement, especially from the mid-20th century onward.
title: Beijing Declaration and Platform for Action is adopted
description: The Fourth World Conference on Women produces a landmark global agenda for gender equality.

timeline_title: Timeline of the Women's Rights Movement
timeline_description: Major legal, intellectual, and activist milestones in the modern women’s rights movement, especially from the mid-20th century onward.
title: UN Security Council adopts Resolution 1325
description: Resolution 1325 places women, peace, and security on the international agenda.

timeline_title: Timeline of the Women's Rights Movement
timeline_description: Major legal, intellectual, and activist milestones in the modern women’s rights movement, especially from the mid-20th century onward.
title: UN Women begins operations
description: The new UN entity consolidates and strengthens institutional support for global gender equality work.

timeline_title: Timeline of the Women's Rights Movement
timeline_description: Major legal, intellectual, and activist milestones in the modern women’s rights movement, especially from the mid-20th century onward.
title: Women’s March mobilizes globally
description: Mass demonstrations across the United States and many other countries become one of the largest single-day protests in modern history.

timeline_title: Timeline of the Women's Rights Movement
timeline_description: Major legal, intellectual, and activist milestones in the modern women’s rights movement, especially from the mid-20th century onward.
title: #MeToo becomes a global movement
description: The viral spread of #MeToo transforms public debate about sexual harassment, abuse, and power.

timeline_description: Scientific discoveries, military uses, civil applications, and arms-control milestones in the Atomic Age.
title: Henri Becquerel discovers radioactivity
description: Becquerel’s work opens the scientific path that eventually leads to nuclear physics and the Atomic Age.

timeline_description: Scientific discoveries, military uses, civil applications, and arms-control milestones in the Atomic Age.
title: Marie and Pierre Curie isolate radioactive elements
description: The Curies identify polonium and radium, expanding the study of radioactive matter.

timeline_description: Scientific discoveries, military uses, civil applications, and arms-control milestones in the Atomic Age.
title: Einstein formulates mass-energy equivalence
description: The relation E=mc² provides a theoretical basis for the enormous energy later released in nuclear reactions.

timeline_description: Scientific discoveries, military uses, civil applications, and arms-control milestones in the Atomic Age.
title: Rutherford proposes the nuclear model of the atom
description: Ernest Rutherford’s atomic model places the atom’s mass in a small central nucleus.

timeline_description: Scientific discoveries, military uses, civil applications, and arms-control milestones in the Atomic Age.
title: James Chadwick discovers the neutron
description: The neutron becomes crucial to later nuclear fission research and reactor design.

timeline_description: Scientific discoveries, military uses, civil applications, and arms-control milestones in the Atomic Age.
title: Nuclear fission is discovered
description: Otto Hahn and Fritz Strassmann identify fission products, with Lise Meitner and Otto Frisch explaining the process.

timeline_description: Scientific discoveries, military uses, civil applications, and arms-control milestones in the Atomic Age.
title: Einstein-Szilard letter warns Roosevelt
description: Scientists urge the U.S. government to consider the military implications of nuclear fission.

timeline_description: Scientific discoveries, military uses, civil applications, and arms-control milestones in the Atomic Age.
title: Chicago Pile-1 achieves first controlled chain reaction
description: Enrico Fermi’s team demonstrates the first self-sustaining nuclear chain reaction.

timeline_description: Scientific discoveries, military uses, civil applications, and arms-control milestones in the Atomic Age.
title: Trinity test is conducted
description: The United States detonates the first nuclear explosive device in New Mexico.

timeline_description: Scientific discoveries, military uses, civil applications, and arms-control milestones in the Atomic Age.
title: Atomic bomb destroys Hiroshima
description: The United States drops an atomic bomb on Hiroshima, killing tens of thousands and transforming warfare.

timeline_description: Scientific discoveries, military uses, civil applications, and arms-control milestones in the Atomic Age.
title: Atomic bomb destroys Nagasaki
description: A second atomic bomb is dropped on Nagasaki three days after Hiroshima.

timeline_description: Scientific discoveries, military uses, civil applications, and arms-control milestones in the Atomic Age.
title: United Nations creates Atomic Energy Commission
description: The UN establishes an early international body to address atomic energy and control proposals.

timeline_description: Scientific discoveries, military uses, civil applications, and arms-control milestones in the Atomic Age.
title: Soviet Union tests its first atomic bomb
description: The Soviet test ends the U.S. nuclear monopoly and accelerates the arms race.

timeline_description: Scientific discoveries, military uses, civil applications, and arms-control milestones in the Atomic Age.
title: United States tests Ivy Mike
description: The first full-scale thermonuclear test marks a new level of destructive power.

timeline_description: Scientific discoveries, military uses, civil applications, and arms-control milestones in the Atomic Age.
title: Eisenhower delivers Atoms for Peace speech
description: The U.S. president promotes peaceful nuclear uses while acknowledging the dangers of atomic weapons.

timeline_description: Scientific discoveries, military uses, civil applications, and arms-control milestones in the Atomic Age.
title: Obninsk begins supplying nuclear electricity
description: The Soviet plant at Obninsk is widely recognized as the first nuclear power station to supply electricity to a grid.

timeline_description: Scientific discoveries, military uses, civil applications, and arms-control milestones in the Atomic Age.
title: International Atomic Energy Agency is established
description: The IAEA is created to promote safe nuclear technology and safeguards.

timeline_description: Scientific discoveries, military uses, civil applications, and arms-control milestones in the Atomic Age.
title: Partial Test Ban Treaty is signed
description: Major powers agree to prohibit nuclear tests in the atmosphere, outer space, and underwater.

timeline_description: Scientific discoveries, military uses, civil applications, and arms-control milestones in the Atomic Age.
title: Nuclear Non-Proliferation Treaty opens for signature
description: The NPT becomes the central international agreement limiting the spread of nuclear weapons.

timeline_description: Scientific discoveries, military uses, civil applications, and arms-control milestones in the Atomic Age.
title: Chernobyl disaster occurs
description: The reactor accident in Soviet Ukraine becomes the world’s worst nuclear power disaster.

timeline_title: Timeline of the History of the Novel
timeline_description: Major works and turning points in the long development of the novel as a literary form across cultures.
title: The Tale of Genji is completed
description: Murasaki Shikibu’s court narrative is often described as the world’s first novel.

timeline_title: Timeline of the History of the Novel
timeline_description: Major works and turning points in the long development of the novel as a literary form across cultures.
title: Boccaccio begins The Decameron
description: The Decameron helps shape later prose fiction through its framed storytelling and social observation.

timeline_title: Timeline of the History of the Novel
timeline_description: Major works and turning points in the long development of the novel as a literary form across cultures.
title: Don Quixote Part I is published
description: Miguel de Cervantes’s work is widely treated as a foundational text of the modern European novel.

timeline_description: Major works and turning points in the long development of the novel as a literary form across cultures.
title: Don Quixote Part II is published
description: The second part deepens Cervantes’s formal innovations and the self-awareness of prose fiction.

timeline_title: Timeline of the History of the Novel
timeline_description: Major works and turning points in the long development of the novel as a literary form across cultures.
title: The Princess of Clèves is published
description: Madame de La Fayette’s work becomes an early landmark of psychological fiction.

timeline_title: Timeline of the History of the Novel
timeline_description: Major works and turning points in the long development of the novel as a literary form across cultures.
title: Robinson Crusoe is published
description: Daniel Defoe’s novel helps define the English prose narrative centered on individual experience.

timeline_title: Timeline of the History of the Novel
timeline_description: Major works and turning points in the long development of the novel as a literary form across cultures.
title: Pamela is published
description: Samuel Richardson’s epistolary novel becomes a major milestone in the rise of the English novel.

timeline_title: Timeline of the History of the Novel
timeline_description: Major works and turning points in the long development of the novel as a literary form across cultures.
title: Tom Jones is published

timeline_title: Timeline of the History of the Novel
timeline_description: Major works and turning points in the long development of the novel as a literary form across cultures.
title: The Castle of Otranto launches Gothic fiction
description: Horace Walpole’s work helps found the Gothic novel as a durable modern subgenre.

timeline_title: Timeline of the History of the Novel
timeline_description: Major works and turning points in the long development of the novel as a literary form across cultures.
title: Sense and Sensibility is published
description: Jane Austen refines the domestic and social novel through irony, structure, and close character observation.

timeline_title: Timeline of the History of the Novel
timeline_description: Major works and turning points in the long development of the novel as a literary form across cultures.
title: Waverley is published

timeline_title: Timeline of the History of the Novel
timeline_description: Major works and turning points in the long development of the novel as a literary form across cultures.
title: Frankenstein is published

timeline_title: Timeline of the History of the Novel
timeline_description: Major works and turning points in the long development of the novel as a literary form across cultures.
title: The Pickwick Papers begins serial publication

timeline_title: Timeline of the History of the Novel
timeline_description: Major works and turning points in the long development of the novel as a literary form across cultures.
title: Madame Bovary is published

timeline_title: Timeline of the History of the Novel
timeline_description: Major works and turning points in the long development of the novel as a literary form across cultures.
title: War and Peace is completed

timeline_title: Timeline of the History of the Novel
timeline_description: Major works and turning points in the long development of the novel as a literary form across cultures.
title: Ulysses is published
description: James Joyce’s novel becomes a defining achievement of literary modernism.

timeline_title: Timeline of the History of the Novel
timeline_description: Major works and turning points in the long development of the novel as a literary form across cultures.
title: The Great Gatsby is published

timeline_title: Timeline of the History of the Novel
timeline_description: Major works and turning points in the long development of the novel as a literary form across cultures.
title: The Lord of the Rings begins publication
description: J.R.R. Tolkien’s trilogy reshapes the modern fantasy novel and its global readership.

timeline_title: Timeline of the History of the Novel
timeline_description: Major works and turning points in the long development of the novel as a literary form across cultures.
title: Things Fall Apart is published

timeline_title: Timeline of the History of the Novel
timeline_description: Major works and turning points in the long development of the novel as a literary form across cultures.
title: One Hundred Years of Solitude is published
